issues: Current result remains below contract target: 0.9397 < 0.94, so objective not met.

Observed repeated runtime_error failures likely stem from torch.compile instability with this model/optimizer stack; current code catches compile failure at compile-time only, not execution-time instability.

Model may be replaced by OptimizedModule after compile; run_single_trial accesses model.whiten/model.head/model.parameters() directly, which is a common breakage point with compiled wrappers and likely source of runtime regressions.
Custom Muon optimizer reshapes gradients with g_eff.shape[0]; assumes trainable params are 4D conv weights only. Any future parameter grouping mistake will hard-fail.

CifarLoader loads cached .pt files directly onto CUDA via torch.load(map_location='cuda'), increasing startup memory pressure and making dataset loading fail on environments with transient CUDA issues.
Warmup trials perform full training with dummy labels, adding large wall-clock remote runtime while not affecting scored time; this is risky for evaluation budget/timeouts.
batch_crop uses masked assignment into uninitialized tensors; while masks should cover all samples, any edge-case mismatch would leave garbage values and be hard to debug.
infer hardcodes split(2000) instead of using loader.batch_size, reducing flexibility and creating hidden coupling.
Preflight contains odd dead-code pattern `label_smoothing if False else 0.16`, suggesting careless edits and increasing maintenance risk.
torch.load uses weights_only=False default and emits warnings; not fatal, but avoidable noise in evaluator logs.
summary: Do not approve: accuracy is still below target and there are clear regression risks causing the observed runtime_error failures.
